title: Diplacrum exiguum
taxonomy: Diplacrum exiguum is a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. It is a type of grass that belongs to the genus Diplacrum. The family Poaceae is a large and diverse group of monocotyledonous plants, which also includes other types of grasses and grains. Diplacrum exiguum is native to the Pacific Islands, particularly in Fiji and other surrounding areas. Its classification is further supported by its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogeny.
uses: Diplacrum exiguum is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Pacific Islands. Its medicinal uses include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Additionally, Diplacrum exiguum is used to treat fever and skin infections. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of these uses.
